heading: Importation safeguard against certain Cuban products
label: 6040 Importation safeguard against certain Cuban products
notes: References in TextSection 902(c) of the Food Security Act of 1985, referred to in subsec. (c), is , which is set out as a note under , Agriculture.section 902(c) of Pub. L. 99–198section 1446g of Title 7

children.heading: Prohibition on import of and dealings in Cuban products
children.label: a Prohibition on import of and dealings in Cuban products

children.text: is or has been located in or transported from or through Cuba; or
is made or derived in whole or in part of any article which is the growth, produce, or manufacture of Cuba.
“The NAFTA rules of origin will not in any way diminish the Cuban sanctions program. … Nothing in the NAFTA would operate to override this prohibition.”.

“Article 309(3) [of the NAFTA] permits the United States to ensure that Cuban products or goods made from Cuban materials are not imported into the United States from Mexico or Canada and that United States products are not exported to Cuba through those countries.”.

children.heading: Restriction of sugar imports
children.label: c Restriction of sugar imports
children.text: The Congress notes that section 902(c) of the Food Security Act of 1985 () requires the President not to allocate any of the sugar import quota to a country that is a net importer of sugar unless appropriate officials of that country verify to the President that the country does not import for reexport to the United States any sugar produced in Cuba.Public Law 99–198

children.heading: Assurances regarding sugar products
children.label: d Assurances regarding sugar products
children.text: Protection of essential security interests of the United States requires assurances that sugar products that are entered, or withdrawn from warehouse for consumption, into the customs territory of the United States are not products of Cuba.
